title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is a fundamental principle in geometry that relates the lengths of the sides of a right triangle. It states that the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

title: Identifying the Sides
text: In the given problem, the hypotenuse is $$13$$ inches, and one of the legs is $$5$$ inches. We need to find the length of the other leg.

title: Setting Up the Equation
text: Using the Pythagorean Theorem, set up the equation: $$5^2+b^2={13}^2$$. What is the next step?

title: Calculating the Squares
text: Calculate the squares of the known sides: $$5^2$$ and $${13}^2$$.

title: Solving for $$b$$
text: After calculating the squares, solve for b: $$25+b^2=169$$. What is $$b^2$$?

title: Finding the Length of the Other Leg
text: To find the length of the other leg, take the square root of $$b^2$$.

title: Final Calculation
text: Calculate the square root of $$144$$ to find the length of the other leg.
